# PCR Notes

Polymerase chain reaction — Kary Mullis, 1983. Three steps per cycle:

1. Denaturation (~95 C) — melt the double helix.
2. Annealing (~55 C) — primers bind.
3. Extension (~72 C) — Taq polymerase copies each strand.

Exponential amplification: n cycles → 2^n copies, in theory. In practice
efficiency drops off after ~30 cycles due to enzyme degradation and
primer-dimer formation.
